title: authentication file sometimes required
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Commands like `podman pull`, `podman run`, and possibly others require authentication file, but `podman build` does not.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podman pull fedora:latest` without an authentication file
2. See error message `Error: stat /var/home/seb/.config/docker/config.json: no such file or directory`
3. Wonder why that happens

**Describe the results you received:**

It seems that some commands require an authentication file, even when the image you are pulling/running is available without any kind of authentication. This is especially strange for images that were built locally but cannot be run without having an authentication file.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I was working on an image with the following (simplified) definition:

```
FROM fedora:latest
RUN echo "hi"
```

I could build this image with something like `podman build --tag something/strange:latest --file that`. The build output clearly shows that the `fedora:latest` image was pulled. `podman image ls` shows:

```
REPOSITORY                         TAG           IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
localhost/something/strange        latest        58f1117bc747  6 minutes ago   159 MB
```

Running the image that was just created complains with the same error message as above that I do not have an authentication file.

```
$ podman run 58f1117bc747 ls -alF /etc
Error: stat /var/home/seb/.config/docker/config.json: no such file or directory
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

AFAIR `docker pull` and `docker run` can be used without an authentication file.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Fri Nov 12 21:25:37 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.4.2-1.fc35.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

I'm using the latest version available from my distro, but have not checked latest main branch. https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#12-anonymous-image-pull-fails-with-invalid-usernamepassword seems related but only discusses invalid credentials, whereas I do not have any authentication file at all.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

This is happening on my physical machine using Fedora Silverblue 35.

comments.body: Thanks for reaching out.  Could you share the debug logs (i.e., via `podman --log-level=debug`)? What does `ls -la /var/home/seb/.config/docker/` return?

Yep sure, `podman pull` shows:

```

`ls -la /var/home/seb/.config/docker/` returns the following:

```
$ ls -la /var/home/seb/.config/docker/
"/var/home/seb/.config/docker/": No such file or directory (os error 2)
```

Not having a `~/.docker` folder might seem strange, but I do not need any kind of credentials on this machine because I only use public or local images. To work around this, I first tried:

```
$ touch ~/.config/docker/config.json
$ podman pull fedora:latest
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Error: initializing source docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest: getting username and password: 1 error occurred:
        * reading JSON file "/var/home/seb/.config/docker/config.json": unmarshaling JSON at "/var/home/seb/.config/docker/config.json": unexpected end of JSON input
```

and then wrote an empty (`{}`) JSON object into that file to get it working:

```
$ cat ~/.config/docker/config.json 
{}
$ podman pull fedora:latest     
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4545346f2a49 done  
Copying config 3059bef432 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
3059bef432ebb91a6a51d8f5cf20b033041dbddb3cab79628c1eb3412cbde0ae
```

Additionally, I tried to reproduce this on another machine (MacOSX w/ Podman 3.4.1) which does seem to work without an authentication file.
Is by any chance the `REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE` variable set on the buggy machine?

Nope `env | grep REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE` returns nothing - however that actually showed me the problem!

Some time ago I followed https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/XDG_Base_Directory to move all dotfiles in my $HOME to their proper XDG paths. Since I'm managing my dotfiles across all machines (most of which have an authentication file), this resulted in me having a `DOCKER_CONFIG` env variable that pointed to that location on a machine without an authentication file. I just fixed that by running:

```
$ unset DOCKER_CONFIG
$ podman pull fedora:latest
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4545346f2a49 done  
Copying config 3059bef432 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
3059bef432ebb91a6a51d8f5cf20b033041dbddb3cab79628c1eb3412cbde0ae
```

Sorry for the trouble & thanks for helping out!

Great catch!  Indeed, Podman is also checking for DOCKER_CONFIG for compatibility reasons.

Thanks for reaching out, glad you're back into a working state.
